tool.name: Run immuneML with any YAML specification
tool.description: Run immuneML analyses using YAML specifications
tool.command:
#if $iml_input cp -r ${iml_input.extra_files_path}/result/* . && (mv repertoires/* . &>/dev/null || :) && rm -rf repertoires && #end if
#set $input_orig_names = [] #if $data_input #for $input in $data_input #set input_orig_names += [str($input.element_identifier)] ([ -e ./"$input.element_identifier" ] && echo "File '$input.element_identifier' already exists in the input folder, skipping." || ln -s $input "$input.element_identifier") && #end for# #end if
cp "$yaml_input" specs.yaml && immune-ml ./specs.yaml ${html_outfile.files_path} --tool GalaxyYamlTool && mv ${html_outfile.files_path}/index.html ${html_outfile} && mv ${html_outfile.files_path}/immuneML_output.zip $archive

param.name: iml_input
param.label: immuneML dataset
param.help: This field accepts ImmuneML datasets, as created by the Create Dataset tool.

param.name: data_input
param.label: Additional files
param.help: This field should include individual repertoire files, metadata files, receptor data, and others.

tool.help:
This Galaxy tool can be used to run any possible YAML-based immuneML analysis in Galaxy.
When you want to run the `ExploratoryAnalysis <https://docs.immuneml.uio.no/latest/specification.html#exploratoryanalysis>`_ instruction, or other analyses that do not have a corresponding Galaxy tool, this generic YAML tool can be used. Note that for most applications it is recommended to use the analysis-specific Galaxy tools (e.g., for `creating datasets <root?tool_id=immune_ml_dataset>`_, simulating synthetic `data <root?tool_id=immuneml_simulate_dataset>`_ or `immune signals <root?tool_id=immuneml_simulate_ligo_events>`_, training machine learning `classifiers <root?tool_id=immuneml_train_ml_model>`_ or `generative models <root?tool_id=immuneml_train_gen_model>`_) These other tools are able to export the relevant output to Galaxy history elements.
See `the immuneML Galaxy documentation <https://docs.immuneml.uio.no/galaxy.html>`_ for more details, tips and useful information.
**Tool output**
This Galaxy tool will produce the following history elements:
- Summary: immuneML analysis: a HTML page that allows you to browse through all results.
- ImmuneML Analysis Archive: a .zip file containing the complete output folder as it was produced by immuneML. This folder contains the output of the instruction that was used, including all raw data files. Furthermore, the folder contains the complete YAML specification file for the immuneML run, the HTML output and a log file.
